author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
On the words of the Gospel, Matt. xi. 25, ‘I thank thee, O Father, Lord of Heaven and Earth, that thou didst hide these things from the wise and understanding,’ etc.

ccel.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

title: On the words of the Gospel, Matt. xi. 25, ‘I thank thee, O Father, Lord of Heaven and Earth, that thou didst hide these things from the wise and understanding,’ etc.

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When the Holy Gospel was being read, we heard that the Lord Jesus exulted in Spirit, and said, “I confess to Thee, O Father, Lord of heaven and earth, for that Thou hast hid these things from the wise and prudent, and hast revealed them unto babes.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thus much to begin with, we find before we pass on further, if we consider the words of the Lord with due attention, with diligence, and above all with piety, that we ought not invariably to understand when we read of “confession” in the Scriptures, the confession of a sinner.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now especial need there was of saying this, and of reminding you, Beloved, of this, because as soon as this word was uttered by the reader’s voice, there followed upon it the sound of the beating of your breasts, when ye had heard, I mean, what the Lord said, “I confess to Thee, O Father.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: At the uttering of these words, “I confess,” ye beat your breasts.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now what means this beating of the breast, but to show that which lies hid within the breast, and to chastise by the visible beating the secret sin?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And why did ye this, but because ye heard, “I confess to Thee, O Father.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Ye heard the words “I confess,” but ye did not consider, who it is that confesses.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But consider now.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If Christ, from whom all sin is far removed, said, “I confess:” confession does not belong to the sinner only, but sometimes to him also that praiseth God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We confess then, whether in praising God, or accusing ourselves.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In either case it is a godly confession, either when thou blamest thyself, who art not without sin, or when thou praisest Him who can have no sin.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But if we consider it well: thine own blame is His praise.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For why is it that thou dost now confess in accusing thyself for thy sin? in accusing thyself why dost thou confess? but because thou art become alive from the dead? for the Scripture saith, “Confession perisheth from the dead, as from one that is not.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If confession perisheth from the dead, he who confesseth must be alive; and if he confesseth sin he hath undoubtedly risen again from death.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now if he that confesseth sin hath risen again from the dead, who hath raised him?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: No dead man can raise himself.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He only was able to raise Himself, who though His Body was dead, was not dead.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For He raised up that which was dead.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He raised up Himself, who in Himself was alive, but in His Body that was to be raised was dead.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For not the Father only, of whom it was said by the Apostle, “Wherefore God also hath exalted Him,” raised the Son, but the Lord also raised Himself, that is, His Body.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whence He said, “Destroy this temple, and in three days I will raise it again.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But the sinner is dead, especially he whom the load of sinful habit presseth down, who is buried as it were like Lazarus.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For he was not merely dead, he was buried also.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whosoever then is oppressed by the load of evil habit, of a wicked life, of earthly lusts, I mean, so that that in his case is true which is piteously described in a certain Psalm, “The fool hath said in his heart, There is no God,” he is such an one, of whom it is said, “Confession perisheth from the dead, as from one that is not.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And who shall raise him up, but He who when the stone was removed, cried out, and said, “Lazarus, Come forth?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now what is to “come forth,” but to bring forth what was hidden?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He then who confesseth “cometh forth.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Come forth” he could not were he not alive; he could not be alive, had he not been raised again.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And therefore in confession the accusing of one’s self, is the praise of God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now one may say, what profit then is the Church, if he that confesseth comes forth, at once raised to life again by the voice of the Lord?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What profit to Him that confesseth, is the Church, to which the Lord said, “Whatsoever ye shall bind on earth, shall be bound in heaven.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Consider this very case of Lazarus: he comes forth, but with his bands.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He was alive already through confession, but he did not yet walk free, entangled as he was in his bands.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What then doth the Church to which it was said, “Whatsoever ye shall loose, shall be loosed;” but what the Lord said forthwith to His disciples, “Loose him, and let him go”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whether then we accuse ourselves, or directly praise God, in both ways do we praise God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If with a pious intention we accuse ourselves, by so doing we praise God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When we praise God directly, we do as it were celebrate His Holiness, who is without sin: but when we accuse ourselves, we give Him glory, by whom we have risen again.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This if thou shalt do, the enemy will find none occasion whereby to overreach thee before the judge.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For when thou shall be thine own accuser, and the Lord thy Deliverer, what shall he be but a mere calumniator?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: With good reason hath the Christian hereby provided protection for himself against his enemies, not those that may be seen, flesh and blood, to be pitied, rather than to be feared, but against those against whom the Apostle exhorts us to arm ourselves: “We wrestle not against flesh and blood;” that is, against men whom ye see raging against you.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They are but vessels, which another uses, they are but instruments which another handles.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “The devil,” saith the Scripture, “entered into the heart of Judas, that he should betray the Lord.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: One may say then, what have I done?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hear the Apostle, “Give not place to the devil.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou hast given him place by an evil will: he entered, and possessed, and now uses thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He had not possessed thee, hadst thou not given him place.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore doth he warn and say, “We wrestle not against flesh and blood, but against principalities and powers.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Any one might suppose this meant against the kings of the earth, against the powers of this world.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How so? are they not flesh and blood?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And once for all it is said, “not against flesh and blood.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Turn thy attention from all men.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What enemies then remain?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Against principalities and powers of spiritual wickedness, the rulers of the world.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It might seem as though he gave the devil and his angels more than they have.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is so, he has called them the “rulers of the world.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But to prevent misunderstanding, he explains what this world is, of which they are the rulers.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “The rulers of the world, of this darkness.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is, “of the world, of this darkness?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The world is full of those who love it, and of unbelievers, over whom he is ruler.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This the Apostle calls darkness.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This darkness the devil and his angels are the rulers of.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This is not the natural, and unchangeable darkness: this darkness changes, and becomes light; it believes, and by believing is enlightened.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When this takes place in it, it will hear the words, “For ye were sometimes darkness, but now are ye light in the Lord.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For when ye were darkness, ye were not in the Lord: again, when ye are light, ye are light not in yourselves, but in the Lord.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “For what hast thou which thou hast not received?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Inasmuch then as they are invisible enemies, by invisible means must they be subdued.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: A visible enemy indeed thou mayest overcome by blows; thy invisible enemy thou conquerest by belief.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: A man is a visible enemy; to strike a blow is visible also.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The devil is an invisible enemy; to believe is invisible also.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Against invisible enemies then there is an invisible fight.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: From these enemies how can any man say that he is safe?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For this I had begun to speak of, but I thought it necessary to treat of these enemies at some little length.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But now that we know our enemies, let us see to our defence against them.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “In praising I will call upon the Lord, so shall I be safe from mine enemies.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou seest what thou hast to do.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “In praising call;” that is, “in praising the Lord, call.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For thou wilt not be safe from thine enemies, if thou praise thyself.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “In praising call upon the Lord, and thou shalt be safe from thine enemies.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For what doth the Lord Himself say?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “The sacrifice of praise shall glorify Me, and there is the way, in which I will show him My salvation.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Where is the way?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In the sacrifice of praise.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let not your foot then wander out of this way.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Keep in the way; depart not from it; from the praise of the Lord depart not a foot, nay, not a nail’s breadth.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For if thou wilt deviate from this way, and praise thyself instead of the Lord, thou wilt not be safe from thine enemies; for it is said of them, “They have laid stumbling-blocks for me by the way.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore in whatever measure thou thinkest that thou hast good of thine own self, thou hast deviated from the praise of God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Why dost thou marvel then, if thine enemy seduce thee, when thou art thine own seducer?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hear the Apostle, “For if a man think himself to be something when he is nothing, he seduceth himself.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Give heed then to the Lord confessing; “I confess to Thee, O Father, Lord of heaven and earth.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I confess to Thee, that is, I praise Thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I praise Thee, not I accuse myself.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now as far as the taking of very man is concerned, all is grace, singular and perfect grace.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What merit had that man who is Christ, if thou take away the grace, even that so pre-eminent grace, whereby it behoved that there should be One Christ, and that He whom we acknowledge should be He?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Take away this grace, and what is Christ but a mere man? what but the same as thou art thyself?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He took a Soul, He took a Body, He took a perfect Man; He uniteth him to Himself, the Lord maketh one Person with the servant.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What pre-eminent grace is this!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Christ in heaven, Christ on earth; Christ at once both in heaven and earth; not two Christs, but the same Christ, both in heaven and earth.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Christ with the Father, Christ in the Virgin’s womb; Christ on the Cross, Christ succouring some souls in hell; and on the self-same day Christ in paradise with the robber who confessed.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And how did the robber attain to this blessedness, but because he held on that way, in which “He showeth His salvation”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: That way, from which let not thy foot wander.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For in that he accused himself, he praised God, and made his own life blessed.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He looked in hope for this from the Lord, and said to Him, “Lord, remember me when Thou comest into Thy kingdom.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For he considered his own wicked deeds, and thought it much, if mercy should be shown him even at the last.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But the Lord immediately after He had said, “Remember me”—when? “when Thou comest into Thy kingdom,” saith, “Verily I say unto thee, Today shall thou be with Me in paradise.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Mercy offered at once, what misery deferred.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hear then the Lord confessing; “I confess to Thee, O Father, Lord of heaven and earth.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What do I confess?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Wherein do I praise thee?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For this confession, as I have said before, signifieth praise.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Because Thou hast hid these things from the wise and prudent, and hast revealed them unto babes.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is this, Brethren?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Understand by that which is opposed to them.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Thou hast hid these things,” saith he, “from the wise and prudent;” and he did not say, thou hast revealed them to the foolish and imprudent, but “Thou hast hid these things” indeed “from the wise and prudent, and hast revealed them unto babes.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: To these wise and prudent, who are really objects of derision, to the arrogant who in false pretence are great, yet in truth are only swollen up, he opposed not the foolish, nor the imprudent, but babes.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Who are babes?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore “Thou hast hidden these things from the wise and prudent.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Under the name of the wise and prudent, He hath Himself explained that the proud are understood, when He said, “Thou hast revealed them unto babes.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore from those who are not babes Thou hast hidden them.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is from those who are not babes?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: From those who are not humble.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And who are they but the proud?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: O way of the Lord!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Either there was none, or it lay hid, that it might be revealed to us.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Why did the Lord exult?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Because it was revealed unto babes.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We must be little babes; for if we would wish to be great, “wise and prudent” as it were, it is not revealed unto us.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Who are these great ones?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The wise and prudent.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Professing themselves to be wise, they became fools.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Here then thou hast a remedy suggested from its opposite.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For if by “professing thyself wise, thou art become a fool; profess thyself a fool, and thou wilt be wise.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But profess it in truth, profess it from the heart, for it is really so as thou professest.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If thou profess it, do not profess it before men, and forbear to profess it before God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: As to thyself, and all that is thine, thou art altogether dark.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For what else is it to be a fool, but to be dark in heart?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He saith of them at last, “Professing themselves to be wise, they became fools.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Before they professed this, what do we find?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “And their foolish heart was darkened.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Acknowledge then that thou art not to thyself a light.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: At best thou art but an eye, thou art not the light.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And what good is even an open and a sound eye, if the light be wanting?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Acknowledge therefore that of thine own self thou art no light to thyself; and cry out as it is written, “Thou, Lord, wilt light my candle: Thou wilt enlighten, O Lord, my darkness with Thy Light.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For myself I was all darkness; but Thou art the Light that scattereth the darkness, and enlighteneth me; of myself I am no light to myself, yea I have no portion of light but in Thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So John also, the friend of the Bridegroom, was thought to be the Christ, was thought to be the Light.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “He was not that Light, but that he might bear witness of the Light.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But what was the Light?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It was the true Light.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is the true Light?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “That which lighteneth every man.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If that be the true Light which lighteneth every man, then it lightened John also, who professed and confessed rightly, “Of His fulness have all we received.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: See if he said ought else, but “Thou, O Lord, shalt lighten my candle.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Finally, being now enlightened, He gave His testimony.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For the benefit of the blind the lamp gave witness to the Day.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: See how that He is a lamp; “Ye sent,” He said, “unto John, and ye were willing for a season to rejoice in his light; he was a burning and a shining lamp.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He, the lamp, that is, a thing enlightened, was lighted that it might shine.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: That which can be lighted can be extinguished also.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now that it may not be extinguished, let it not expose itself to the wind of pride.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore, “I confess to Thee, O Father, Lord of heaven and earth, because Thou hast hid these things from the wise and prudent,” from those who thought themselves to be light, and were darkness; and who because they were darkness, and thought themselves to be light, could not even be enlightened.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But they who were darkness, and confessed that they were darkness, were little babes, not great; were humble, not proud.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Rightly therefore did they say, “O Lord, Thou wilt lighten my candle.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They knew themselves, they praised the Lord.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They did not stray from the way of salvation; “They in praise called upon the Lord, and they were saved from their enemies.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Turning then to the Lord our God, the Father Almighty, in purity of heart, let us render unto Him, as our frailty best can, our highest and abundant thanks, with our whole mind praying His singular goodness, that in His good pleasure He would vouchsafe to hear our prayers, that by His Power He would drive out the enemy from our deeds and thoughts, would enlarge our faith, direct our minds, grant us spiritual thoughts, and bring us safe to His endless blessedness, through His Son Jesus Christ.
